scope: FQ-20 (merged per operator): painted chrome sliced from the blueprint mockup + dock command center + direct-manipulation HUD editing. No inventory drag-drop, no modal-panel chrome, no touching concurrent Codex lanes.
summary: The HUD now wears the actual rendered art from the operator's blueprint mockup instead of approximations: a deterministic slicer extracts thirteen painted assets (module frames with a corner medallion, chip frame, riveted dock plate, slot frames, glyph buttons, and both ornate orb rings with their glass punched so the live liquid shows through) into a new ui_painted asset lane with its own light verification contract. Every HUD surface consumes them with intact fallback chains, and the mini-map finally has a real border - the ornate frame as a NinePatch overlay. The dock became the command center the operator asked for: the five module toggles are chips inside it, the corner toolbar is gone, and chip state mirrors reality in both directions. HUD editing is now direct manipulation - grab any panel and drag it, pull the gold corner grip to stretch it continuously between half and double size, with locks removed and a live overlay showing outlines and grips. Layout persistence moved to v3. Verified end to end: 318/318 smoke, clean strict audit, 208-PNG verifier pass, validator/doctor green, rendered captures reviewed with two polish fixes applied. Mid-run, a concurrent Codex session wrote opening cels and player-gear overlays outside its agreed wiki scope; those were excluded from the commit and flagged.
acceptance: Thirteen painted chrome assets sliced deterministically and consumed with fallbacks; audit shows all thirteen LIVE
Mini-map and every module wear painted borders; contextual entries and command chips wear the chip frame
Module open/close is managed from chips inside the dock; corner toolbar removed; two-way sync verified (fq20_dock_command_center)
Edit mode: direct drag + continuous corner-grip resize with clamps, overlay, and v3 persistence (fq17_hud_edit_direct_manipulation)
Isolated waited Godot smoke 318/318; strict audit clean; pixel verifier 208 PASS
validation: rendered tour 1280x720 -> regions reviewed; ornate content margin + medallion clip polish applied
independent sonnet review -> recorded in the ledger before commit
decisions: The blueprint mockup is the recyclable art source the operator pointed at; slicing it deterministically keeps the assets regenerable and reviewable
Frame interiors rebuild from mirrored clean edge strips so baked mockup text can never leak into a 9-slice
Orb extraction is geometric (mask disks + measured glass punch), never a color flood - the flood eats the dark iron
ui_painted is a separate lane with a light contract; painted renders cannot honor the 16-color pixel contract and should not pretend to
The dock is the single command center: chips inside it own module visibility; the corner toolbar is retired
Locks are gone - edit mode itself is the gate; resize is continuous (0.5x-2.0x) via the corner grip
Concurrent Codex writes outside the wiki (opening cels, player gear) are excluded from this commit and flagged to the operator
risks: fq09u1_live_clip_switch remains timing-flaky on cold isolated profiles (one flake this run; clean on re-run) - hardening belongs to the music lane
Painted chrome is only as good as the mockup slice boundaries; future mockup revisions require re-running the slicer and re-reviewing
Codex exceeded its wiki-only scope mid-run; its opening/gear files are unreviewed and uncommitted
next_action: Operator reviews the painted chrome captures; scope FQ-21/22 (modal-panel chrome, Diablo-1 drag-drop inventory); review the out-of-scope Codex writes in their own lane.
